ucs.title: [LLM] APT12 / IXESHE Office-exploit spearphish chain (Equation Editor / Word → script-host child)
ucs.description: Hunts APT12's signature delivery: a spearphish attachment (RTF/DOC/XLS) that exploits a memory-corruption flaw in Office (e.g. CVE-2012-0158, CVE-2014-1761, CVE-2014-4114) and chains into a script-host or LOLBin child to drop the IXESHE / Etumbot / RIPTIDE backdoor. Correlates inbound mail-with-attachment to the recipient device's process tree within a tight window.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_hash) as hashes from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","eqnedt32.exe","outlook.exe")) (Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","mshta.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","hh.exe")) by host user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename firstTime as ChildSpawnTime | join type=inner host [ | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as MailTime values(All_Email.file_name) as attachment values(All_Email.src_user) as sender from datamodel=Email.All_Email where All_Email.file_name IN ("*.doc","*.docx","*.rtf","*.xls","*.xlsx","*.ppt","*.pps") by All_Email.recipient | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Email)` | rex field=recipient "(?<user>[^@]+)"  ] | where ChildSpawnTime >= MailTime AND (ChildSpawnTime - MailTime) <= 1800 | table ChildSpawnTime MailTime host user sender attachment parent_process_name process_name cmdline hashes
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let WindowSec = 1800;   // 30 min from inbound mail to child-spawn — APT12 lures often need a click-through
let SuspectMail = EmailEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EmailDirection == "Inbound" and DeliveryAction == "Delivered"
    | join kind=inner (
        EmailAttachmentInfo
        | where FileType in~ ("DOC","DOCX","RTF","XLS","XLSX","PPT","PPS","PPSX")
        | project NetworkMessageId, AttachName = FileName, AttachSHA256 = SHA256, FileType
      ) on NetworkMessageId
    | project NetworkMessageId, MailTime = Timestamp, SenderFromAddress, RecipientEmailAddress,
              Subject, AttachName, FileType, AttachSHA256;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","eqnedt32.exe","outlook.exe")
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","mshta.exe",
                       "regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","certutil.exe",
                       "bitsadmin.exe","hh.exe")
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| extend RecipientEmailAddress = AccountUpn
| join kind=inner SuspectMail on RecipientEmailAddress
| where Timestamp between (MailTime .. MailTime + WindowSec * 1s)
| project SpawnTime = Timestamp, MailTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, MailTime),
          DeviceName, AccountUpn, SenderFromAddress, Subject, AttachName, FileType,
          AttachSHA256,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          Child = FileName,
          ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildSHA256 = SHA256
| order by SpawnTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let WindowSec = 1800;
let SuspectMail = OfficeActivity
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where OfficeWorkload == "Exchange"
    | where Operation in~ ("Send","MailItemsAccessed","Receive")
    | where isnotempty(ItemAttachments)
    | extend AttachLower = tolower(tostring(ItemAttachments))
    | where AttachLower has_any (".doc",".docx",".rtf",".xls",".xlsx",".ppt",".pps")
    | project MailTime = TimeGenerated, RecipientUpn = MailboxOwnerUPN,
              Sender, Subject, AttachLower, MessageId;
let ProcessChain = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where ParentProcessName has_any ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","eqnedt32.exe","outlook.exe")
    | where NewProcessName has_any ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe",
                                    "mshta.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe",
                                    "certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","hh.exe")
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend RecipientUpn = tolower(strcat(SubjectUserName, "@", SubjectDomainName))
    | project SpawnTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, RecipientUpn, Account,
              ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine;
ProcessChain
| join kind=inner SuspectMail on RecipientUpn
| where SpawnTime between (MailTime .. MailTime + WindowSec * 1s)
| project SpawnTime, MailTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', SpawnTime, MailTime),
          Computer, RecipientUpn, Sender, Subject, AttachLower,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine
| order by SpawnTime desc
ucs.rationale: APT12 / IXESHE / Numbered Panda's first-stage delivery is documented across Trend Micro and FireEye reports as malicious Office attachments (RTF/DOC/XLS) exploiting Office stack-overflow CVEs (CVE-2012-0158, CVE-2014-1761, CVE-2014-4114) — eqnedt32.exe spawning script hosts is the diagnostic byproduct of the Equation-Editor exploit chain that they reused for years. Tying the recipient mailbox to t

ucs.title: [LLM] APT12 DNSCALC / Etumbot dead-drop resolver beacon (web-service C2 from non-browser process)
ucs.description: Hunts APT12's namesake DNSCALC technique: malware (IXESHE / Etumbot / RIPTIDE) fetches an attacker-controlled page on a legitimate web service (Wikipedia, blogspot, WordPress, Pastebin, Dropbox, Google Docs) and decodes the C2 IPv4 from numeric strings embedded in the page, then connects to that derived IP. Detection chains a non-browser, non-Office process making HTTP/HTTPS to a known dead-drop service with a near-time outbound TCP connection from the same process to a public IP not present in the org's 30-day DNS baseline.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count values(Web.url) as urls values(Web.dest) as deaddrop_dest min(_time) as DeadDropTime from datamodel=Web.Web where (Web.dest IN ("*.blogspot.com","*.wordpress.com","*.pastebin.com","raw.githubusercontent.com","gist.githubusercontent.com","docs.google.com","www.dropbox.com","dl.dropboxusercontent.com","en.wikipedia.org")) (Web.app NOT IN ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","iexplore.exe","brave.exe","opera.exe","safari.exe","outlook.exe","teams.exe","slack.exe","onedrive.exe","code.exe","git.exe","curl.exe")) by host Web.user Web.app Web.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Web)` | join type=inner host app [ | tstats summariesonly=true count values(All_Traffic.dest_ip) as derived_ip min(_time) as BeaconTime from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_category!="internal" All_Traffic.dest_port IN (80,443,8080,8443) NOT (All_Traffic.dest_ip="10.0.0.0/8" OR All_Traffic.dest_ip="172.16.0.0/12" OR All_Traffic.dest_ip="192.168.0.0/16") by host All_Traffic.app All_Traffic.dest_ip | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` ] | where BeaconTime >= DeadDropTime AND (BeaconTime - DeadDropTime) <= 600 | table host user app deaddrop_dest urls derived_ip DeadDropTime BeaconTime
ucs.kql:
let Lookback = 7d;
let BaselineDays = 30d;
let CorrelationSec = 600;     // 10-min window: fetch dead-drop, decode, then call out
let DeadDropDomains = dynamic([
    "blogspot.com","wordpress.com","pastebin.com","raw.githubusercontent.com",
    "gist.githubusercontent.com","docs.google.com","www.dropbox.com",
    "dl.dropboxusercontent.com","en.wikipedia.org","medium.com",
    "sites.google.com","plus.google.com"]);
let KnownBrowsers = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","iexplore.exe",
    "brave.exe","opera.exe","safari.exe","outlook.exe","teams.exe","slack.exe",
    "onedrive.exe","code.exe","git.exe","acrobat.exe","acrord32.exe"]);
// 30-day baseline of public IPs the org has seen — APT12's DNSCALC IPs are first-of-kind
let IpBaseline = DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp between (ago(BaselineDays) .. ago(Lookback))
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | summarize by RemoteIP;
// Stage 1 — non-browser process touches a dead-drop web service
let DeadDropFetch = DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | where isnotempty(RemoteUrl)
    | where RemoteUrl has_any (DeadDropDomains)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (KnownBrowsers)
    | where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
    | project DropTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessId,
              InitiatingProcessSHA256, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              DeadDropUrl = RemoteUrl;
// Stage 2 — same process beacons out to a public IP not in the 30-day org baseline
let Beacon = DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | where ActionType in ("ConnectionSuccess","ConnectionAttempt")
    | where RemotePort in (80, 443, 8080, 8443, 53)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (KnownBrowsers)
    | join kind=leftanti IpBaseline on RemoteIP
    | project BeaconTime = Timestamp, DeviceId,
              InitiatingProcessId, InitiatingProcessFileName, RemoteIP, RemotePort;
DeadDropFetch
| join kind=inner Beacon on DeviceId, InitiatingProcessId, InitiatingProcessFileName
| where BeaconTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + CorrelationSec * 1s)
| project DropTime, BeaconTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', BeaconTime, DropTime),
          DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          Process = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ProcessSHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          ProcessCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          DeadDropUrl, DerivedC2 = RemoteIP, RemotePort
| order by BeaconTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let Lookback = 7d;
let BaselineDays = 30d;
let CorrelationSec = 600;
let DeadDropDomains = dynamic([
    "blogspot.com","wordpress.com","pastebin.com","raw.githubusercontent.com",
    "gist.githubusercontent.com","docs.google.com","www.dropbox.com",
    "dl.dropboxusercontent.com","en.wikipedia.org","medium.com"]);
let KnownBrowsers = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","iexplore.exe",
    "brave.exe","opera.exe","safari.exe","outlook.exe","teams.exe","slack.exe",
    "onedrive.exe","code.exe","git.exe","acrobat.exe","acrord32.exe"]);
let IpBaseline = ImNetworkSession
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(BaselineDays) .. ago(Lookback))
    | where ipv4_is_private(DstIpAddr) == false
    | summarize by DstIpAddr;
let DeadDropFetch = ImWebSession
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where isnotempty(Url)
    | extend Host = tolower(HttpHostName)
    | where Host has_any (DeadDropDomains)
    | where ActingProcessName !in~ (KnownBrowsers)
    | where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
    | project DropTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
              ActingProcessName, ActingProcessId, DeadDropUrl = Url;
let Beacon = ImNetworkSession
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where ipv4_is_private(DstIpAddr) == false
    | where DstPortNumber in (80, 443, 8080, 8443, 53)
    | where ActingProcessName !in~ (KnownBrowsers)
    | join kind=leftanti IpBaseline on DstIpAddr
    | project BeaconTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActingProcessName, ActingProcessId,
              DerivedC2 = DstIpAddr, DstPortNumber;
DeadDropFetch
| join kind=inner Beacon on DvcHostname, ActingProcessName, ActingProcessId
| where BeaconTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + CorrelationSec * 1s)
| project DropTime, BeaconTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', BeaconTime, DropTime),
          DvcHostname, ActorUsername, ActingProcessName,
          DeadDropUrl, DerivedC2, DstPortNumber
| order by BeaconTime desc
ucs.rationale: DNSCALC is APT12's signature dynamic-resolution trick (named for the group's CrowdStrike alias DNSCALC and documented by FireEye/Mandiant in the Etumbot/IXESHE write-ups): the implant pulls a benign-looking page from a public web service, parses numeric strings embedded by the operator, computes the four octets of the real C2 IP, then connects directly to that IP — bypassing standard DNS-based IOC

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `ActingProcessName` is not a column of ImNetworkSession, ImWebSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
`ActorUsername` is not a column of ImNetworkSession, ImWebSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImAuthentication, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
`ActingProcessId` is not a column of ImNetworkSession, ImWebSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
